subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: governance/rights/politics rebuilt from independent sources (IMF Art IV, World Bank, V-Dem, RSF, Freedom House, HRW, OHCHR, ICG, ISS); Tanzanian state media EXCLUDED there, with economics admitted via IMF.
A solid managed economy dragged down by a sharp 2025 authoritarian turn: the long-ruling party barred and charged opposition leaders and the October-2025 election brought a deadly crackdown.
Standout drags: a sharp 2025 democratic reversal, a reversed press opening, and weak healthcare; well-anchored inflation, solid growth, and a young population are offsets.

economic.notes: A solid, well-managed growth economy with anchored inflation and a diversifying resource base.

fiscal_state.notes: Debt ~49% GDP and falling, a ~3% deficit, on-track under an IMF program. (IMF Art IV)

monetary_independence.notes: A managed, reasonably stable shilling with limited central-bank autonomy and some FX-shortage pressure. (IMF; BoT)

inflation.notes: ~3.3-3.6% and well-anchored - a relative strength. (IMF REO)

banking_stability.notes: Sound-ish, with strong credit growth, developing and shallow. (BoT; IMF)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Gold (a top African producer), tourism, and a major LNG-export project ahead; diversifying but commodity-exposed. (BoT; PwC)

institutional.notes: Party dominance with no judicial independence and a reversed press opening.

rule_of_law.notes: Party dominance with no judicial independence and non-bailable treason charges deployed politically. (Freedom House 2026; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: IMF-engaged and usable, with legacy data-credibility concerns. (IMF Art IV)

civil_service_capacity.notes: Developing, with mixed delivery capacity. (World Bank)

press_freedom.notes: RSF fell sharply, the post-2021 reopening reversed with 2025 suspensions and arrests. (RSF 2026)

political_social.notes: A sharp 2025 authoritarian turn, with a young population the offset.

political_stability.notes: A party in power since 1961; the Oct-2025 election barred the main opposition and charged its leader, with a deadly lockdown and post-election violence. (Freedom House 2026; HRW; OHCHR)

civil_liberties.notes: A sharp 2025 reversal - forced disappearances, lethal force on protesters, and mass treason charges. (Freedom House 2026)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A refugee host under encampment; not a relocation destination. (UNHCR/HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: A legacy of national cohesion, but a Zanzibar-union and 2025-electoral cleavage. (Freedom House)

demographics.notes: Very young and fast-growing - a dividend-or-strain double-edge. (World Bank; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A non-aligned pragmatic balancer, with jihadist-spillover risk from the south.

alliance_reliability.notes: Non-aligned, pragmatically balancing China, the Gulf, and the West. (ISS)

conflict_involvement.notes: No interstate war, with Cabo Delgado jihadist spillover into the south and troops deployed. (ISS; ICG)

physical_practical.notes: Improving large infrastructure projects, against weak healthcare and drought/flood exposure.

climate.notes: Rain-dependent agriculture, with most disasters drought/flood and rising flood frequency. (World Bank; GIZ)

healthcare.notes: Low spend and weak infrastructure, with improving basics. (WHO)

infrastructure.notes: Improving (a major hydropower dam now live, a railway, ports) but with still-poor power access. (African Energy)

summary: A solid, well-managed growth economy - low and falling debt, well-anchored inflation, a diversifying gold/tourism/LNG base, and a young population - dragged down by a sharp 2025 authoritarian turn. The party that has ruled since 1961 barred the main opposition and charged its leader, and the October-2025 election brought a deadly crackdown and post-election violence; the post-2021 press reopening has been reversed. Weak healthcare, drought/flood exposure, and still-poor power access weigh on the physical side, with jihadist spillover from the south a geopolitical tail. The outcome distribution tilts negative.
